plainEnglish: If your pages offer help mechanisms—contact details, a help link, a chat widget—and they repeat across pages, keep them in the same relative location and order on every page. Users in difficulty shouldn't have to hunt for help in a different spot each time.
whyItMatters: Users with cognitive disabilities who need help can't find it if the contact link or help widget moves to a different place on each page.
quickCheck: Compare pages and confirm help mechanisms (contact link, chat, help) appear in the same relative order and location on each.
commonMistakes: Placing the help or contact link in a different spot on different pages.
Showing a chat or help widget on some pages but not others, inconsistently.
Reordering help options relative to other content between templates.

codeExamples.label: Help link in the same footer slot on every page
codeExamples.code:
{/* Rendered in the same place site-wide */}
Contact support

codeExamples.label: Help link placed inconsistently across pages
codeExamples.code:
{/* In the header here, buried mid-page elsewhere */}
Contact support
